reviews.review_msg: Creates fair, functional, and AI-proof assignments made for first and second years to explore assignment types in university. Lectures were very reflective of textbook content, and he was more than happy to talk with students.
Very accessible outside of class, is extremely caring and kind! His lectures can be a bit boring and hefty but it's easy to do good in his class.
Super dry and boring lectures. He takes forever to grade assignments
attendance is not mandatory, the class is very easy and engaging
Loved his class, attendance was not mandatory, and class structure was great! 3 exams over the sem rather than midterm and final

I was a bit worried about this class because I thought there would be a lot of writing, but most assignments were done in tutorials and lectures. If you follow the instructions, it's easy to get a good grade!!!!

Assignment heavy course, but most of them take place in tutorial so as long as you attend, there isn't much work to be done outside of class which was great. Lectures not mandatory except on certain days. Very kind prof who wants to engage students and start conversations in the classroom.
I absolutely loved Professor Leavitt. His lectures were engaging, he explained concepts if asked, and his tests are not designed to trick you. If you attend lectures, you will succeed.
I love him. I don't care who disagrees. I love him. He's very caring and very understanding I've had many unfortunate situations and he was always so understanding of those and helped me. I love him.

Awesome prof, lectures weren't mandatory but Dr. Leavitt made them interesting if you did go. Exams every two weeks, but all questions were based off of lecture, pretty much only used the textbook for the online quizzes. Absolutely recommend his class!!
Made the class so easy

Very kind professor, he's clear about what he expects you to know for the exams and you can tell he's passionate about what he teaches. I thought the overall course was easy, his tests were not too bad it was MC and short answers but he'd state exactly what would be the topic/s for the short answers.

This is my second class with Prof Leavitt and it's been great. 4 exams and no final but each exam is based on 2 chapters and is easy to study for. Didn't go to a single lecture, just followed along on his lecture slides. There is also an easy assignment basically worth as much as an exam, and he tells you the written portion on the exam. Recommend

Peter was an amazing prof, exams were mainly on lecture content and slides were very detailed. He told us about the short answer before the exams which was very helpful. There were two surveys which he gave a few bonus marks for and it seemed he genuinely cared for his students. Super happy I took him, I'd say take him if you get the chance!

His lectures can get boring, but he is awesome! If you know the material (lec slides or textbook), you will do extremely well on the exams. I didn't study a lot for the exams and did pretty well. The exams asked fair questions, and nothing felt extremely difficult. One assignment which is marked easily. He tells you the main short question topic.
If you know his lecture slides you get an A.
Lectures are clear, and exams are fair. If you show up and do a little work, you'll do well.

Peter has been the best prof I've ever had so far. His lectures are really engaging and it seems he truly loves teaching psych and talking to students. Although I didn't do great on the exams, I think they were pretty fair and he made it clear what would be on them. Whatever you do, take a class with Peter!!!!
I had Peter for psych102 and he is absolutely amazing.

Amazing professer Never came to class and I still did decent Leavitt is one of the most generous professors and he genuinely cares about his students even though there's 400 and tends to everyone and cares about his job

I've heard PSYC300w is a nightmare. With Peter, easiest psyc 300 course I've taken. He's so nice, cares for students, reformatted the class to make it easier. Not as much writing as I thought, and the writings itself are easy. Lectures are pointless for what you're marked on. Don't need to go. Just follow assignment instructions. Easy & low effort!
Amazing class! There is lots of work to do, but if you keep up with your work and try your best - he sets you up for success!
Dr.Leavitt lowkenuinely chill ash. 10/10 class

I have taken multiple classes with Dr. Leavitt, and he is by far the best professor I have ever had. He is approachable, easy to understand and willing to take on feedback. On occasion, his assignment descriptions were missing some criteria, though this was cleared up before the deadline, or he gave full marks for that part of the grade.

TAKE HIM IF U CAN!! I didn't even use the textbook and still got a pretty good grade and I also didn't go to class… but uhhh his slides have so much info that u will be fine skipping class!! He goes into so much detail in class and also he's very passionate abt his work and also he's js a very nice person that is rly easy to talk too abt anything!!

This was the 2nd course I took with Dr. Leavitt, and throughout both he's been kind, supportive of students, engaging in lectures, and accessible outside of class. All his exams have been fair. Lecture slides are based on the textbooks but I still recommend going as he expands on concepts well. & doing the readings will make the exams a lot easier.
Amazing Professor, not a difficult grader or gives difficult syllabus.

I found prof to be super nice, and very clear at explaining terminology. The tests are weighted heavy, but most of the material in the lecture covered it. I read the textbook for the most part, but it wasn't necessary to pass, the exams were straightforward. Overall, while lectures are long the time is used well, and he was a very good teacher!

He is a very open and nice professor. The lectures can be long, but he tries to make them engaging and really cares that we take something from his course. Assignments are straightforward, and he provides examples to guide you through them. His exams are based on his slides, and he has pretty easy weekly quizzes. He is great for a first year!

Might be the best professor of all time. He made the course super engaging with his lectures and all the assignments were easy + do-able! He actually responds to feedback and answers questions in a helpful way. The exams are mostly based on lectures, while the online quizzes were based on the textbook. Also, he posts lecture slides before class!
Dr. Leavitt's is the best prof I've had this semester. He's so clear in lectures and his grading is straight forward. I hope this man is living the best life ever.
Can tell he really cares about what he teaches and if you understand it. Great teacher super great with any questions!
Super chill prof!

He's really easy going. I never actually went to class except assignment and exam days. He requires the textbook but exams are generally based off of his lecture slides. Get your hands on a textbook though because you will specifically need it to complete one of the major assignments.

I hated how this class was organized, you told how to do assignments after they were done, the entire class is based around one group project that we were all blindly stumbling through. His lectures very boring but the final was super easy. Honestly I was just annoyed with this class all the time.

Peter Leavitt is the best professor at SFU. His lectures are engaging, his TA's marking is very fair, his exams are only based on lecture slides, he is a sweetheart. Take this course with him if you are able to, there is quite a bit of material, but if you attend the lectures and put the work in, you will receive a great grade!

The course is very lecture-heavy, with 3 exams totaling just over 50% of the grade. If you don't do well on the exams, then you're kind of screwed. Attendance isn't mandatory, and you can just teach yourself the material on your own time. Overall, he's a super nice guy, and you can tell he loves teaching.

10/10. Clearly explains everything, lectures are funny and is passionate about what he's teaching. Had to buy a textbook, but it was used throughout the course. Exams and assignments were well explained in lectures. Cares that you understand and that you enjoy learning :)

Very nice professor, and pretty straight forward class. Do not need to read textbook, all exam questions were pulled from the slides. Quizzes are open book and he tells you what the written part of the exam will be on. Def Recommend the class.
Amazing amazing amazing prof! Very understanding, very caring + amazing lectures. He is very approachable, too! Genuinely not a single bad thing to say about his class. The BEST prof at SFU!!

Peter is the best. He's super nice and caring, and you can tell he loves what he does. He's approachable and helps with any question. His tests are easy because he basically tells you the written questions before and MC is based off lecture. Assignments are straightforward. He really tries to keep the class engaged, even when it's a 3-hour lecture.

Amazing!! as a first year uni student he has been the best intro professor by far. I recommend 100 times over, the quizzes were open book and the exams were based exactly on the slides. Most of the time I studied the night before and still did very well.

Peter is one of the nicest profs I've ever had. He genuinely cares about his students and is always open to chatting during breaks or after class. His exams are fairly easy and are entirely based off the slides which are easy to follow. Lectures can be boring sometimes but that's probably just because I'm not a psyc major lol. Def recommend!

Know his slides to heart, that's where all the midterm information is. Quizzes are mostly based on textbook, but are open book. Super nice prof and genuinely cares when you stay for the whole lecture, might get boring sometimes but psychology isn't interesting for everyone! Take him for grade booster but only if you put in the effort to study.

He was very friendly and approachable, and gives the MOST DETAILED study guide to finals and midterms. marks are given on various different assignments which was helpful.The class was lecture based, and if you aren't skilled in stats it may be a bit smart to invest some extra time. overall, great prof, 100% recommend!

I loved taking PSYC100 with Peter. He was really nice. His lectures were full of information, but he was really good at explaining concepts. He goes pretty fast through the slides, but he's very open to questions during class and gets the class involved.

I just finished my semester with Dr. Leavitt. I think he was a phenomenal professor for an intro to psych course! The course content was simplified perfectly through his slides, and he clearly cares about each and every student in his lecture! I would 100% take his class, all the TA's are nice as well.

He makes things easy to understand. The exam material is mostly taught in lecture/on the slides. As long as you're paying attention in class and review the slides couple times you'll be fine. The assignments are pretty easy to do and straightforward. Would definitely take him again.

Peter is absolutely incredible and sweet! His lectures/slides are directly from the textbook, so if you don't want to buy the textbook, you don't have to. His lectures are boring, but assignments, quizzes, and tests are all fair and quite easy. He wants his students to pass, and he is very clear about his grading. It's a GPA booster for sure.

He is so caring and understanding, and one of the best profs at SFU. His exams are mostly from the lecture slides, which are really clear and easy to understand. The assignments throughout the semester are also pretty easy to get full marks on. Definitely take psychology with him if you can.

201W with Peter was surprisingly easy but still a lot of work. Super textbook heavy, and quizzes for each chapter. Lectures weren't super valuable, as slides come from the textbook, so I'd read those instead. Lots of papers throughout the term, and you primarily work with a group, which can be a hit or miss. Nice guy, but a bit boring.

Dr. Leavitt has been my favourite prof at SFU, I took Psyc 100 and 102 with him. He is the most consistent prof and is very reasonable. Weekly quizzes allow for a grade booster and exams are straight forward if you study his slides. All smaller projects are easy to get full marks on and final assignment isn't too heavy.

Super kind and caring Prof, really passionate about Psychology overall, quizzes and exams are easy as long as you've read the textbook. Assignments are generally straightforward. Loves answering questions during lectures so dont be afraid to speak up!

Peter is a good prof but doesn't expand on slides, most of what he says is just read directly off the power point. He's very enthusiastic and understanding!! Exams are not difficult if you study properly.

Hes great at lecture, makes it fun and enjoyable. If you miss a lecture the slides are posted and are mostly what he talks about. I find it good to read the chapter before you go to lecture that way the content is easier to understand. There is no TUT, but lots of studying for midterms and finals. Make sure to go to lecture.

Really sweet prof, truly cares about the students and will take time to talk to them. Really tries to make class engaging through class discussions. 3 exams non-cumulative and weekly open book quizzes. Covers a lot of material but isn't too unbearable with his jokes and consistent breaks. Passionate about gen AI and often brings it up.

Dr. Leavitt is by far the best prof ever. His lectures are so entertaining and fun, even though there's quizzes every week. I would definitely take his class again. You will have so much fun learning with him.

Great prof and great guy! He's funny, very caring and wants to get to know as many students as possible. Loves questions and gives clear instructions. Quizzes + Assignments are easy. Tests do count for a lot, but everythings in the textbook and his lecture slides are useful! Research participation is important for him! Take his class if you can!!!
Very nice prof, he really cares about teaching and will help you if you needed help.

Professor Leavitt was amazing! He has a true passion for psychology, which is clear by the way he speaks about the lecture content. He is kind and available after class to help with any topics you are unclear about. His exams are very easy and based almost entirely on the lecture slides, so I wouldn't bother buying the textbook. 10/10, no notes!

Super easy class! Assignments are fairly easy they are basically used as grade boosters. Lots of chances for bonus marks. Exams are short and easy. The written portion of the exam, the topic is given to you prior. Overall, such a grade booster class and attendance nor is reading the textbook needed to get a good grade.

I love Peter yall he's really straightforward and everything on his lectures are on the test; there's no curveballs or anything unexpected of the sort. He clearly outlines his expectations and all you have to do is follow them- they're really easy to follow. Amazing down to earth guy.

Where do I begin? Petes is the real deal. Super caring, responds to emails fast, and always happy to clear up any confusion. Exam-wise, they were on the easier side, and he let you know what the written question subject would be. Excluding the APA assignment, the written assignment was engaging and not that long (~1000 words over the semester).

Peter is an understanding and caring prof. This was my first psychology course and I'm now looking to pursue it as a major because of his inspiration! The way he taught the PSYC 100 was pretty straightforward and his lectures were interesting. If you want to or need to take a psyc class, take it with him!!

I think honestly, this is an assignment heavy class which should not be surprising because it is a writing class lol. Other than that, I think everything was quite fair because he gives a bunch of assignments and the final paper is only 20% of your mark. He was super easy to reach, and I think if you put in effort you will do great!

300 with Dr. Leavitt was amazing! People say the course is hard, but his first time teaching it went so well. Grading is super fair as there's lots of small easy assignments, and final paper's only 20%! Attendance is mandatory, but tutorials with TA Samantha Mason are the best, she lets you leave an hour early and is hilarious. Highly recommend!

I crammed the entire thing overnight and still got a B so... No excuses guys. He's pretty funny from like the 2 times I talked to him. Also you SHOULD keep up with the lectures because there is a research report to write... Since it's his first time hosting 201W some parts were rough and not clear, but he fixed those mistakes.
Dr Leavitt was absolutely phenomenal. Very approachable, kind, respectable, and wants you to succeed. Every assignment and the final is fair. Don't need to read textbook, his slides cover it all.

Dr. Leaving was a great prof! If im being honest i only attended one lecture and still ended up w a great mark! His tests are so easy- you don't really need the textbook since his lectures cover everything, but just make sure you study the slides throughly and you'll do fine. A big paper at the end too! Otherwise loved the class

Lectures were really boring. I found it more effective to stay at home and read the textbook and take notes off of that. I liked his writing assignments though, it was straight forward and an easy garde booster.

Dr. Leavitt is awesome. His lectures are entertaining and comprehensive and he posts slides so if you can't attend its fine. We a group project, but the final research report on the group project was individual, so your not totally screwed if you get bad group members. The final was super fair too, he wants students to do well and won't trick you.

Professor Leavitt was honestly one of the nicest profs I think I've ever had. I took PSYC 100 as an elective and just to try it, and he was such a caring and easygoing teacher. Everyone was mean to him in his lectures because he's too nice, he deserves so much more credit for how patient he is. His grading is super fair, and he is awesome.

Amazing, he was literally an angel. Exams were so easy I would be shocked after every one of them. The only thing I did to prepare for exams was read the textbook, and I ended up with a 98 in the class. Also, for 2 of the exams, he decided some questions were "too hard," so he gave everyone points. Take this class if you want a great GPA booster!!!

Probably the best prof I've had at SFU so far. He's so nice and always replies to emails. His exams are also really fair and they're exactly like the ppt slides. This class is a great gpa booster I highly recommend taking psyc 100 with him.
Overall, he is an amazing prof. Lectures are a bit lengthy but thorough and straightforward. He truly cares and follows through on feedback.

didn't even go to all the classes, memorized lectures and read the textbook and ur set for exams. Bro is acc the GOAT. He's so nice and helpful and cares about his students and wants to make life as easy as possible for them. Definitely take him if you need psyc 100 but rmbr to work hard for the writing assignments and write clearly and in APA

his lectures are based off the textbook but you need to read the texbook for exams and quizzes. Memorize the lectures and read the textbook and you should be fine. He replies to emails even on weekends and is super helpful even during the actual exam when you ask for help. His exams are scenerio based questions that are really easy to understand.

Know how to write clearly and LEARN APA for this class. Peter makes such nice exams . They are straightforward and directly from lectures but he will throw in some small things from the textbook. READ THE TEXTBOOK FOR THE MIDTERMS YALL Majority of it is from lectures. Quizzes u gotta study but not horrible. Bro might be the best psyc prof yet

Dr. Leavitt is by far the best professor I've had at Sfu. He's so kind, his lectures were always super clear and very helpful for exams. He's also always happy to help and actually legitimately wants to support his students in every way. Never hesitate to take anything psych courses with him if you have the chance.

Dr Leavitt is my favourite prof I have ever had!! He is so kind, gives amazing and clear feedback, and replies to emails and questions so quick, he clearly cares about his students. He doesn't over talk or yap, just enough to understand. Tests were super fair and not difficult! Overall HIGHLY recommend him as a psych prof, he is amazing!!!

Dr Leavitt was very approachable and good at engaging the class during lectures. The exams were always reasonable, though the final paper had somewhat unclear instructions. Overall, a good experience.

Lectures stick to the textbook with little elaboration (except on AI). Exams are easy but occasionally tricky due to wording on 1-2 written questions. While he's passionate about teaching and engages students, discussions sometimes go off-topic. Overall, he's a solid choice for first-year students since he's straightforward and understanding.

Great prof, lectures are simple and interesting. The no midterms or finals, just an exam per 4 units. He is very understanding and as long as you are honest and reach out for help in time, he will do what he can. Make sure to read your chapters weekly, it will help. The chapters are pretty long but detailed, the textbook is not difficult to read.

I am currently taking my second class with this professor; he has been excellent so far. He is clearly very passionate about what he is teaching, which is difficult to find with introductory courses. There is a lot of reading in his class but that can be said for any introductory psychology or social sciences class.

Dr. Leavitt engages with students during lectures, makes them a little more interesting, and shows he cares about his students. his slides highlight what will be on the exams but mainly if you read the textbook and study, it shouldn't be hard to do well on the exams. he's pretty clear about what will be on the exams & there aren't any surprises.

Nice guy,lectures are boring. Just read the textbook/notes and you will do good on the exam. Attend the review days he will sometimes mention/hint what some of the short answer questions will be. Obsessed with AI.

He cares for his students and is passionate! Tedious lectures, though he tries his best to engage the class and take feedback. I studied off the textbook for in-depth learning, however, you can probably survive just off the slides. Going to lectures can help but skipping and studying on your own is more time efficient. Overall, recommend 👍
Really nice professor, if you read the textbook the class is easy.  The TAs didn't grade very well, but if you email them they'll always find extra points to give you, so it's a bit suggestive.

the class layout is amazing and very simple. 3 tests, non cumulative, each consisting of 2-3 chapters. Lectures were very boring and the slides are very weirdly worded so id recommend asking chat gpt to reword them into simpler terms. Also lock in for the writing assignment, that's where u can lose marks. Def a gpa booster class.

Dr. Leavitt spoke passionately about the topics. The textbook was straightforward but the chapters were really long (around 50-80 pages). There was no final, just 3 exams with MC and short answers. The content was not hard to understand, but what made it hard was how much content was covered. Just read the textbook and you'll do fine.

For the written portions, the TAs were very vague in the feedback and it was toughly graded. Midterms were fair with a few multiple choice being too difficult. He was very passionate and wanted to hear from everyone!

Although reading your textbook is not mandatory, his slides are really condensed. So reading the textbook is really good to clarify some theories especially since his exams can be confusing as they go beyond than just what he says they (the exam topics) are on. There are weekly quizzes that I found to be great grade boosters. Great prof though!

The lectures are long, but prof is passionate so it helps. Tests are straightforward and textbook is long (but useful). TA's, hard markers with no feedback. Weekly quizzes helps with reviewing material. The paper was reasonably lengthed and quite short compared to other courses. Great prof and definitely recommend if you want/need to take PSYC100!

He talks a lot during lectures and often goes off topic so we don't finish reviewing the slides. Sometimes, he chats with the students with questions for a long time and not finish the slides either. Other than that, he's really nice and offered to mark easy because of confusing exam questions!

He's a nice guy but lectures are extremely boring. He never elaborates on his notes and moves fast "just go back and read on your own time." His exams are easy and are based off his notes/textbook but were sometimes worded really weirdly and he would have to give us bonus marks for his mistakes often. He's also weirdly obsessed with AI.

Don't go to lectures just read the textbook and study from that and you"ll be fine
Quizzes are open book and based all on textbook material 
Exams are combo of textbook/lecture 
One-note, boring, unengaging speaker
Obsessed with AI 
Some questions on exams needed a lot of clarification and were confusing to everybody

Really nice guy who cares about students. Reasonable and generous marker on assignments/tests. No final exam, just 3 midterms and a short paper. He was a very passionate lecturer and made topics relevant and interesting. He uses a textbook, but I don't think it is necessary for success as long as you pay attention and study lectures.

Really nice guy, got the vibe he actually cares about students. Passionate about topics, and is really good at making information relevant. Generous and reasonable with test/assignment grading. I recommend the textbook if you want to go above and beyond, but as long as you go to lectures and pay attention, you will do well.
Standard psych class, read the textbook and do well. Going to lecture and reading notes is probably just as effective
